summary: Verified fix for Profile page ReferenceError bug (user -> me) reported in Arabic. Full frontend regression on bottom nav 'حسابي' for both super_admin and freshly signed-up creator. All flows PASS with no React error boundaries or console errors.

test_results.admin_btn_visible_super_admin (open-admi: PASS - لوحة المدير button visible with red gradient
test_results.regression_bottom_nav (feed/workspace/ex: PASS - no pageerror / React errors during navigation
test_results.admin_btn_hidden_creator: PASS - open-admin-btn NOT rendered (role=creator, gated correctly)
test_results.quick_access_btns_creator (crm/content/t: PASS - all three visible for profile owner

critical_code_review_comments: Profile.jsx (306 lines) still contains many responsibilities (services CRUD, videos grid, earnings card, admin/CRM/Content/Tasks quick-access, add-service modal). Consider splitting into subcomponents (ProfileHeader, ProfileQuickActions, ProfileServicesTab, AddServiceModal) if it keeps growing.
In Layout.jsx goProfile() reads user.username without null-safety on username (only null-checks user). Low risk since backend guarantees username on login, but defensive `?.` recommended.
Profile.jsx follow/removeSvc use empty catch blocks (silent errors). Consider surfacing failures via toast for better UX debugging.
Profile.jsx api.get calls in load() don't have error handlers; a 404 on any of the three would leave profile=null and show endless 'جارٍ التحميل...' spinner. Add error state + toast.
test_credentials: Super Admin: crm@test.com / testpass123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Created ephemeral test creator: creator_<timestamp>@test.com / testpass123.
seed_data_creation: One fresh creator account created via /auth signup multi-step wizard (name -> Test Creator, username -> creator<ts>, email -> creator_<ts>@test.com, role -> creator).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Profile.jsx bug (undefined `user` var -> ReferenceError) is fixed and verified. Bottom-nav 'حسابي' (data-testid=nav-profile) now navigates to /u/<username> and renders data-testid='profile-page' with correct role-gated content. Signup is a multi-step wizard: step 1 fills [input-name, input-username, input-email, input-password] + click [next-step]; step 2 select role via [data-testid=role-creator] + [next-step] to complete. Auth submit btn is [data-testid=submit-auth]. Console shows no pageerrors during profile/feed/workspace/explore navigation.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: In /app/frontend/src/pages/Profile.jsx line 144, the JSX referenced `user?.role === 'super_admin'` but the destructured variable from useAuth() is aliased to `me` (line 10: `const { user: me, logout } = useAuth();`). `user` was undefined in scope. Since JSX conditional rendering evaluates the expression during render, this threw ReferenceError: user is not defined, crashing the whole Profile component. React's error boundary (or lack thereof) showed a blank/error page. FIX applied by E1: replaced `user?.role` with `me?.role`. VERIFIED: profile-page element renders, admin button conditionally shown only for super_admin, and creator profile works without the admin button. No further mitigation needed.
